questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 110-3-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: When was the email sent?
options: Nov. 13, 2021 ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 34-38 題 When we were young, adults always said: “Do good to others and good will come back to you.” Is it true or merely a saying? Let’s see what people said. I am a Japanese college student. Few years ago, I went to Negros Island, Philippines as a “Habitat for Humanity” volunteer. I was assigned to build a house with many people from different countries, including Filipinos, Americans, Indians, and Koreans. The mission was tough! However, on the last day, when all of us stood in front of the house we had built, a sense of fulfillment just came to us. From the task, besides the “ exotic bond ” with foreign friends, I learned a sense of goodwill: the locals value everything in life; they generously offered food, space, and hearts in a way that I was unfamiliar with, and so on. These are often forgotten in a wealthy country like Japan. In this materialistic world, being good to others is dangerous sometimes. I remembered once when I kindly gave the directions to a stranger, unfortunately, in return he almost kidnapped me. From then on, I knew I might be kind to my friends or family, but not to others. I am a homemaker. Last Friday afternoon, when I was waiting for a bus, I saw a baby crying in her mother’s lap. I did not know the baby nor the young mother, but I went to them, making the baby smile. I could find the mother’s anxiety faded away instantly. She gifted me a smile with a hearty “thank you.” That moment made me content and happy. With joy, I returned home and shared the story with my family. After listening to my roadside incident, my family wore a smile as the beautiful one on the baby’s face that gave each of us the dopamine power! Doing good may give a certain degree of satisfaction and benefits to the doer, but it is not guaranteed “what goes around comes around.” Nevertheless, once we have done it, we shall make a difference to the world.
questions.explanation: 此封電子郵件何時發送? (B) 艾曼達和但丁對做好事有不同的看法。 (A) 2021 年 11 月 8 日 (C) 但丁同意做好事會帶來滿足和好處。 (B) 2021 年 11 月 10 日 (D) 作者相信行善可以改變世界。 (C) 2021 年 11 月 12 日

options: Having a satisfying and joyful mood.

Knowing how to protect someone. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 39-42 題 We are in the COVID-19 pandemic, and we have no idea when this is going to end. To understand the situation, the KFF (Kaiser Family Foundation) did a survey on March 11-15, 2020 and surprisingly found that there were some differences in how men and women respond to the pandemic. First of all, more women than men said that their lives had been bothered by the pandemic (49% vs. 40%). Next, women, compared with men, were more likely to stay home instead of going to work or other regular activities (81% vs. 69%). Women would rather choose to change or cancel their travel plans than men (72% vs. 66%) or to shelter in the current location (88% vs. 76%). In fact, when the outbreak became more severe (two weeks after the first poll taken), KFF did a second survey—one sex gap has gotten strikingly larger, particularly among parents. Women overall were 16 percentage points more likely than men to say that worry or stress related to coronavirus had a negative impact on their mental health. Among parents of children under age 18, the sex gap reached a striking 25%. In the poll taken just two weeks prior, namely, in the first survey, the sex gap among all adults was 9 percentage points (36% vs. 27%) and among parents, it was just 5 percentage points (36% vs. 31%). To conclude, women are more likely to worry about both the health and economic effects of a pandemic on their families, and the worry would become even more if they have children under 18.
questions.explanation: 正解：(A)；來源：110-3-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.q: Regarding the pandemic impact on people’s health and economy, which of the following charts is correct?
options: 二、非選擇題 ( 第 I 題,每格 2 分,共 4 分;第 II 題到第 III 題,每題 6 分。共 16 分 ) I. 填充 說明: ▲請依據中文提示,將試題內空格  、  答案之完整單字 ( 含提示之字首 ),分別作答於答 案卷之作答欄  、  之指定範圍內以完成句子。 ▲請勿抄題,每格限填一字,超過一字者視為錯誤,不予計分。
questions.explanation: 關於疫情對人們健康和經濟影響,下列哪張圖表是正 能適切表達原文題意,文法、標點、用字遣詞 3 確的? 僅有少數不當之處。 說明:在倒數第二句提到,第一次調查,所有成年人 僅能部分表達原文題意,文法、標點、用字遣 2 的女性與男性性別比例是 36% 對 27%, 在父母的女性 詞有多處失當之處。 與男性比例是 36% 對 31%,第二次調查,有孩子的男 無法表達原文題意,文法、標點、用字遣詞僅 1 女差距是 25%,又最後一句說女性多於男性,因此數 有少數正確之處。 字多是女性,反之亦然,用刪去法,故答案選 (B) (1) 空白 ( 未書寫文字 ) 。 (2) 文字瑣碎、形同未答。 二、非選擇題 0 (3) 只抄題目或其他試題。 I. 填充 (4) 內容完全離題或錯誤。
